response:
Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class

We can create a UdpClient instance and send a packet to the server

We can measure the time taken to send the packet using the DateTime.Now property

We can then calculate the difference between the current time and the time the packet was sent

We can then compare the time taken to send the packet with the time taken to receive the packet

This way we can get the one direction time

We can also use the NetworkInformation.GetConnectionTimeAsync method to get the time taken to send the packet

This method returns the time taken to send the packet in milliseconds

We can then compare this time with the time taken to receive the packet

This way we can get the one direction time

We can also use the NetworkInformation.GetConnectionTimeAsync method to get the time taken to send the packet

This method returns the time taken to send the packet in milliseconds
